100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On July 13, 1926, the world was abuzz with significant developments. In Britain, the General Strike showed signs of waning as negotiations continued between labor leaders and the government. Meanwhile, in the United States, the ongoing debate over immigration policies intensified, with calls for stricter regulations gaining traction. In Europe, tensions remained high as nations grappled with economic recovery following the Great War.
